One of the things I've noticed on the blogs that, and maybe it's because some of these are a little older, but I still see the intelligent routing come through a lot on blog posts as something that people should do.
And this is like, I was proving a couple of posts today, and there was one on AI trends, and it talked about intelligent routing.
And there was another one on how to improve auth rates that talked about intelligent routing, and one on how to choose a gateway that talked about intelligent routing.
Intelligent routing basically means telling our customers to send transactions to another processor instead of us.
Like, we don't want to do that.
The only time that we would ever talk about intelligent routing is

In the context of speaking to a payment orchestration platform about becoming one of the acquirers that they route transactions to, we wouldn't in the context of talking to customers say, hey, configure intelligent routing on Rapids platform and send it to the acquirer with the best authorization rates.
We are the acquirer.
We're not trying to have them intelligently route things.
We're going to an orchestration platform that does routing, and then we're trying to be one of their acquirers.
So we should just like figure out a way to like not have that come up as a feature or capability or suggestion in blogs.

And then another just general conundrum that I'm seeing on the blogs, and Kristin, I'd like you to chime in on this, because you probably have seen some of these blogs now, too, and actually the rest of the team is like, when it gets into some of these topics, I think it's happening intentionally because you're trying to differentiate the content.
Like, it'll be a blog post about what is a payment gateway.

And, you know, instead of just saying, like, here's what a payment gateway is and providing a diagram of, like, what a payment gateway does, it goes into, like, here's how to choose a payment gateway to maximize your revenue.
But then in doing that, it goes really deep into, like, advanced capabilities that, like, a payment gateway can offer or things that you could do.

And generally, when it does that, it's no longer talking about what Rapid is either capable of, like, it's actually basically just, like, going off the rails into, like, hypothetical things or capabilities that we don't offer.
And so I see that somewhere, it's just like, it kind of like, it'll go really deep into a topic, but it kind of like veers off of what Rapyd really does.

And so I think there's some situations where it's great to talk like blue sky about things from an SEO standpoint, but not when you're talking about like, you know, advanced AI capabilities within fraud management systems that like far exceed what we would ever do.
Do you see that, Kristin, too?
